name: Butter Horn Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 Cake compressed yeast            1/2 cup Melted shortening
OR 1  cake dry yeast               1/4 cup Sugar
4 to 4 1/2 cups flour              1 cup Milk, scalded
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften the yeast in lukewarm milk. If compressed yeast is used, allow it to stand for 5 minutes. If dry yeast is used, allow it to stand for 30 minutes.
2. Add salt, shortening, sugar, and eggs to the yeast mixture. Gradually add flour, beating thoroughly after each addition until the dough is just stiff enough to knead on a lightly floured board.
3. Knead the dough until smooth and elastic. Cover with a damp cloth and set in a warm place to rise until doubled in bulk (the dough made with dry yeast will need to rise overnight in a warm place).
4. Allow the dough to rise again. Turn onto a lightly floured board. Roll out into a circular shape.
5. Cut into sections, each about 3 inches wide at the outer edge. Begin at the outside and roll each section toward the center. Form into crescents.
6. Place in well-oiled baking pans. Cover and let rise until tripled in bulk.
7. Bake in a hot oven (450°F) for 12-15 minutes. Makes 16 rolls.
